subject: Career Technology
content_standard: B7.1.1.1 Demonstrate knowledge of basic concept of staying healthy [cite: 2432]
indicator_description: Explain the need to stay healthy [cite: 2432]
examplars:
1. Explain what is meant by staying healthy. E.g. Staying healthy: physical, mental, and social wellbeing, and as a resource for living a full life-exercise the body, have enough rest, eat a balanced diet, avoid drug abuse and negative peer pressure [cite: 2432]
2. Discuss and present in groups the consequences of not taking good care of one's body E.g. Contract disease and fall ill. [cite: 2432]
3. Research and write on materials and strategies (ways) used for improving personal hygiene and discuss, in groups. [cite: 2432]

subject: Career Technology
content_standard: B7.1.1.1 Demonstrate knowledge of basic concept of staying healthy [cite: 2432, 2438]
indicator_description: Explain what is meant by food hygiene [cite: 2438]
examplars:
1. Explain what is meant by food hygiene. E.g. Conditions and measures needed to ensure safety of food from production to consumption. [cite: 2438]
2. Research into food hygiene practices, in groups and report in class for discussion. E.g. Proper storage and preservation of food. [cite: 2438]

subject: Career Technology
content_standard: B7.1.1.1 Demonstrate knowledge of basic concept of staying healthy [cite: 2432, 2438]
indicator_description: Describe ways of maintaining personal hygiene [cite: 2438]
examplars:
1. In groups, discuss ways of maintaining personal hygiene. E.g. Wash the body often. Clean the teeth at least twice a day. Wash hands after visiting the toilet. [cite: 2438]
2. Demonstrate the following in groups. E.g. Care of finger nails, hair, nose, ear, mouth and teeth [cite: 2438]

subject: Career Technology
sub_strand: 1.2 PERSONAL, WORKSHOP AND FOOD LABORATORY SAFETY
content_standard: B7.1.2.1 Demonstrate knowledge of preventing accidents in the workshop and food laboratory [cite: 2443]
indicator_description: Describe accidents in the workshop/food laboratory [cite: 2443]
examplars:
1. Explain what is meant by accidents. E.g. Accidents in the workshops are injuries that occur in the workshop unexpectedly. [cite: 2443]
2. Discuss the types of accidents that occur in the workshop. E.g. Falls, cuts, bruises and explosions. [cite: 2443]
3. Predict the causes of accidents that can occur in the workshop/food laboratory. E.g. Tiredness/fatigue, poor lightening and ventilation. [cite: 2443]

subject: Career Technology
sub_strand: 1.2 PERSONAL, WORKSHOP AND FOOD LABORATORY SAFETY
content_standard: B7.1.2.1 Demonstrate knowledge of preventing accidents in the workshop and food laboratory [cite: 2443, 2449]
indicator_description: Explain the need for keeping the workshop and the food laboratory safe [cite: 2449]
examplars:
1. Identify personal safety measures in the workshop/food laboratory. E.g. Proper use of personal protective equipment in the workshop and laboratory and adherence to safety rules and regulations. [cite: 2449]
2. Discuss how to keep tools and equipment safe to prevent accidents in the workshop /food laboratory. E.g. Proper storage of food, materials, tools and equipment. [cite: 2449]
3. Demonstrate ways of preventing accidents in the workshop/food laboratory. E.g. Follow instructions and do not rush through work; Good lighting and ventilation, work systematically and carefully; Keep oneself from harm, observe safety precautions, wear personal protective equipment (PPE). [cite: 2449]

subject: Career Technology
content_standard: B7.1.3.1 Demonstrate knowledge of basic concept of Environmental Health [cite: 2454]
indicator_description: Discuss the constituents/pillars of environmental health [cite: 2454]
examplars:
1. Identify the constituents/pillars of environmental health, in groups. E.g. Disease control, clean water, sanitation and hygiene. [cite: 2454]
2. Discuss the causes of environmental health using ICT tools and other sources and report in class. E.g. Air, water and soil pollutions, chemical exposures [cite: 2454]
3. Research the consequences of poor environmental health, in groups and present for class discussion. E.g. Transmission of diseases such as cholera, diarrhoea, dysentery, hepatitis A, typhoid and polio and exacerbates stunting [cite: 2454]

subject: Career Technology
content_standard: B7.1.3.1 Demonstrate knowledge of basic concept of Environmental Health [cite: 2454]
indicator_description: Explain the preventive measures of environmental health [cite: 2454]
examplars:
1. Identify preventive measures of poor environmental health. E.g. Avoid polluting water bodies; Avoid littering; Avoid defecating indiscriminately [cite: 2454]
2. Present findings in groups. [cite: 2454]
3. Undertake a project in tree planting around the school/community. [cite: 2454]

subject: Career Technology
content_standard: B7.2.1.1 Demonstrate knowledge of basic concept of compliant materials [cite: 2460]
indicator_description: Describe compliant materials [cite: 2460]
examplars:
1. Explain what is meant by compliant materials. E.g. Compliant materials are materials that have recognised, predictable and consistent properties such as paper/card, fabric/textiles. [cite: 2460]
2. Sort out compliant materials from the variety of available materials. [cite: 2460]
3. Write a summary of the explanation and sorting. [cite: 2460]

subject: Career Technology
content_standard: B7.2.1.1 Demonstrate knowledge of basic concept of compliant materials [cite: 2460]
indicator_description: Distinguish between types of compliant materials [cite: 2460]
examplars:
1. Classify the various compliant materials under their types: Paper, Card, Fabric/Textile [cite: 2460]
2. Give examples of each class of compliant materials: Paper-copy paper, construction paper; Card-solid white board, corrugated card; Fabric/Textile-cotton, nylon [cite: 2460]
3. Read out the summary of the lesson. [cite: 2460]

subject: Career Technology
content_standard: B7.2.1.1 Demonstrate knowledge of basic concept of compliant materials [cite: 2460, 2467]
indicator_description: Explain how compliant materials are obtained [cite: 2467]
examplars:
1. Search for information on how paper/card is obtained, in groups. E.g. Paper is made from wood; a tree is felled, broken into chips, chips are boiled in water and chemicals added to form pulp; the pulp is pressed with rollers. [cite: 2467]
2. Find information from books and other sources on how fabric/textile is obtained, in groups. E.g. Fabric/Textile is obtained from natural and man-made fibres which are turned into yarns and threads. [cite: 2467]
3. Write out findings and present in class. [cite: 2467]

subject: Career Technology
content_standard: B7.2.2.1 Demonstrate knowledge of basic concept of resistant materials [cite: 2471]
indicator_description: Describe resistant materials [cite: 2471]
examplars:
1. Explain what is meant by resistant materials. E.g. Resistant materials are materials that are not pliable or flexible and cannot be easily compressed with bare hands (plastic, wood, metal, ceramics, glass). [cite: 2471]
2. Sort out resistant materials from the variety of available materials E.g. plastic, wood, metal, ceramics, glass and their composites [cite: 2471]
3. Write down the summary of the explanation and sorting. [cite: 2471]

subject: Career Technology
content_standard: B7.2.2.1 Demonstrate knowledge of basic concept of resistant materials [cite: 2471]
indicator_description: Distinguish between the types of resistant materials [cite: 2471]
examplars:
1. Sort out different resistant materials into various categories: Plastics-thermosetting plastics and thermoplastics; Wood-hardwoods, softwoods and manufactured (man-made) boards; Metals-ferrous, non-ferrous, alloys and smart. [cite: 2471]
2. Write the various types of resistant materials under their categories and present in class for discussion. [cite: 2471]

subject: Career Technology
content_standard: B7.2.2.1 Demonstrate knowledge of basic concept of resistant materials [cite: 2471, 2478, 2483, 2488]
indicator_description: Explain how each of the resistant materials is obtained [cite: 2478]
examplars:
1. Discuss the two main sources (natural and synthetic) from which plastics are obtained. [cite: 2478]
2. Find out information from different sources including online, on the two types of plastics and give examples. E.g. Thermoplastics: polythene, PVC, nylon; Thermosetting plastics: urea formaldehyde, polyester resin. [cite: 2478]
3. Make a table and match products to the types of plastics they are made from [cite: 2478]
4. Explain briefly how wood is obtained. E.g. A mature living tree is felled, the branches are cut off to obtain the log, which is then converted (sawn) to standard sizes, then seasoned. [cite: 2483]
5. Distinguish between solid timber and man-made boards and give examples. [cite: 2483]
6. Compare the weight of products made from solid timber and man-made boards. [cite: 2483]
7. Explain briefly how metals are obtained. E.g. The raw material is mined from the earth; it undergoes processes such as crushing, washing and grading. [cite: 2483]
8. Identify products made from each category of metals. [cite: 2483]
9. Identify and classify materials used for building. E.g. Natural: sand, stones, clay; Man-made: cement, lime [cite: 2488]
10. Discuss the sources of the natural building materials. E.g. Sand is obtained from pits, river banks; Stone is obtained from quarries. [cite: 2488]

subject: Career Technology
content_standard: B7.2.3.1 Demonstrate understanding of the properties of smart and modern materials [cite: 2492]
indicator_description: Explore the general properties of smart and modern materials [cite: 2492]
examplars:
1. Describe smart and modern materials. E.g. Smart materials have properties that can be significantly changed by external stimuli. Modern materials are developed through invention to have improved properties. [cite: 2492]
2. Identify the main factors that affect the properties of smart and modern materials. E.g. Light, temperature, moisture. [cite: 2492]
3. Describe the effects of light on smart and modern materials and products. E.g. Photochromic materials change colour in response to light. [cite: 2492]
4. Explain the effects of temperature on smart and modern materials and products. E.g. Thermochromic materials change in colour depending on temperature. [cite: 2492]
5. Discuss how moisture affects products made from smart and modern materials and products. E.g. Graphene oxide based materials bend when exposed to moisture. [cite: 2498]

subject: Career Technology
content_standard: B7.2.4.1 Demonstrate knowledge of basic food commodities [cite: 2504]
indicator_description: Discuss food commodities [cite: 2504]
examplars:
1. Explain what is meant by food. E.g. Edible substance used by the body to maintain life. [cite: 2504]
2. Explain what is meant by food commodities. E.g. Ingredients needed to produce different varieties of food. [cite: 2504]
3. Give examples of common food commodities in the community. E.g. fish, cassava, okro, orange, milk, meat [cite: 2504]
4. Classify food commodities under the two main sources, i.e. plant source and animal source [cite: 2504]
5. Discuss reasons for eating food; e.g. to satisfy our hunger, build body, provide heat energy, protect body from diseases. [cite: 2504]

subject: Career Technology
content_standard: B7.3.1.1 Demonstrate understanding of measuring and marking out tools and equipment for production [cite: 2508]
indicator_description: Identify and use measuring and marking out tools and equipment for production and classify them [cite: 2508]
examplars:
1. Identify the type of measuring and marking out tools and equipment used in various workshops (Food lab, Sewing, Building, Wood, Metal). [cite: 2508]
2. Think-write-pair and share at plenary session, the uses of each tool and equipment. [cite: 2508]
3. Discuss the importance of measuring and marking out tools. E.g. Accuracy, avoidance of waste of material. [cite: 2514]
4. Identify from displayed realia or pictures, tools and equipment used for measuring and marking out. [cite: 2514]
5. Sketch and label parts of measuring and marking out tools and display sketches for appraisal. [cite: 2514]
6. Demonstrate how to use the measuring and marking out tools and equipment. [cite: 2514]

subject: Career Technology
content_standard: B7.3.1.1 Demonstrate understanding of measuring and marking out tools and equipment for production [cite: 2508, 2514]
indicator_description: Demonstrate how to care for and maintain measuring and marking out tools and equipment used for production [cite: 2514]
examplars:
1. Share experiences from home on how to care for tools and equipment. [cite: 2514]
2. Discuss cleaning agents/materials used to clean and maintain tools based on the respective material (e.g. Silvo, Brasso, oil). [cite: 2514]
3. Demonstrate how to care for measuring and marking out tools and equipment according to the material used in making them. [cite: 2514]

subject: Career Technology
content_standard: B7.3.2.1 Demonstrate understanding of cutting/shaping tools and equipment for production [cite: 2519]
indicator_description: Identify cutting and shaping tools and equipment used for production. [cite: 2519]
examplars: 1. Identify the displayed cutting tools for Food lab (knives, cutters), Sewing (scissors, shears), Woodwork (saws, chisels), Building (bolster, hammer), Metal/plastics (saws, files). [cite: 2519]

subject: Career Technology
content_standard: B7.3.2.1 Demonstrate understanding of cutting/shaping tools and equipment for production [cite: 2519]
indicator_description: Use appropriate skills in cutting, chopping, slicing, dicing and shaping products [cite: 2519]
examplars:
1. Demonstrate the appropriate techniques in cutting, chopping, slicing and dicing in food production. [cite: 2519]
2. Demonstrate the appropriate techniques in cutting, paring, moulding in wood, metal and building. [cite: 2519]

subject: Career Technology
content_standard: B7.3.2.1 Demonstrate understanding of cutting/shaping tools and equipment for production [cite: 2519, 2525]
indicator_description: Demonstrate how to care for, and maintain cutting and shaping tools used for production [cite: 2525]
examplars:
1. Share experiences on how to care for, and maintain cutting and shaping tools. E.g. oil metal parts, wash mould box. [cite: 2525]
2. Identify cleaning agents used to clean tools and equipment according to the material used. [cite: 2525]
3. Demonstrate how to care for cutting and shaping tools and equipment according to the material used in making them and discuss in class. [cite: 2525]

subject: Career Technology
content_standard: B7.3.3.1 Demonstrate understanding of joining and assembling materials, tools and equipment used for production [cite: 2530]
indicator_description: Identify joining and assembling materials, tools and equipment used for making artefacts/products [cite: 2530]
examplars:
1. Identify joining and assembling materials, tools and equipment used for Sewing, Food production, Woodwork, Metal work, Plastic, Building, and Paper. [cite: 2530]
2. Research for more joining and assembling materials using ICT tools and other sources and discuss in groups. [cite: 2530]
3. Sketch/make an album of joining and assembling materials, tools and equipment. [cite: 2530]
4. Display the sketches for appraisal. [cite: 2530]

subject: Career Technology
content_standard: B7.3.3.1 Demonstrate understanding of joining and assembling materials, tools and equipment used for production [cite: 2530, 2536]
indicator_description: Use appropriate skills for joining and assembling artefacts using the materials, tools and equipment [cite: 2536]
examplars: 1. Demonstrate the appropriate techniques in using joining and assembling materials and tools in various areas: stitching in sewing; boiling/stewing in food; nailing/gluing in wood; soldering in metal; bonding in building. [cite: 2536]

subject: Career Technology
content_standard: B7.3.4.1 Demonstrate knowledge and understanding of the Kitchen Essentials [cite: 2541]
indicator_description: Explain what is meant by kitchen essentials [cite: 2541]
examplars: 1. Explain what is meant by basic kitchen essentials. E.g. Kitchen essentials are indispensable/vital tools and equipment needed/necessary for meal preparation and service. [cite: 2541]

subject: Career Technology
content_standard: B7.3.4.1 Demonstrate knowledge and understanding of the Kitchen Essentials [cite: 2541]
indicator_description: Demonstrate skills in the classification of kitchen essentials [cite: 2541]
examplars:
1. Classify and discuss kitchen essentials according to sizes (Small, Large, Mechanical). [cite: 2541]
2. Present responses on the classified kitchen essentials for appraisal. [cite: 2541]

subject: Career Technology
content_standard: B7.3.5.1 Demonstrate knowledge of finishes and finishing [cite: 2547]
indicator_description: Identify finishes and finishing applied to products/ artefacts [cite: 2547]
examplars:
1. Explain what is meant by finishing and finishes. E.g. Finishing is the final surface treatment to improve beauty and protection. [cite: 2547]
2. Identify types of finishes (e.g. lacquer, paint, vanish, tiles). [cite: 2547]
3. Identify solvents used to thin finishes (e.g. thinner, turpentine, water). [cite: 2547]
4. Finishing of food by garnishing and cake decoration. [cite: 2547]
5. Discuss ways of applying finishing to food products. [cite: 2547]
6. Identify the types of garnishes (Savoury, Sweet). [cite: 2547, 2554]
7. Finishing in sewing using edge finishes. [cite: 2554]
8. Identify edges of articles that are finished. E.g. Neckline, armhole. [cite: 2554]

subject: Career Technology
sub_strand: SIMPLE STRUCTURES AND MECHANISMS, ELECTRIC AND ELECTRONIC SYSTEMS
content_standard: B7.4.1.1 Demonstrate understanding of structures in construction [cite: 2560]
indicator_description: Outline the uses of structures in construction [cite: 2560]
examplars:
1. Explain what is meant by structures in construction. E.g. Method of supporting loads to prevent collapse. [cite: 2560]
2. Identify and classify structures under natural and man-made. [cite: 2560]
3. Classify structures under frame and shell. [cite: 2560]
4. Discuss the uses of structures in construction. E.g. Carrying loads, supporting parts. [cite: 2560]
5. Make sketches of both frame and shell structures and prepare photo albums. [cite: 2560]
6. Display photo albums for appraisal. [cite: 2560]

subject: Career Technology
sub_strand: SIMPLE STRUCTURES AND MECHANISMS, ELECTRIC AND ELECTRONIC SYSTEMS
content_standard: B7.4.1.1 Demonstrate understanding of structures in construction [cite: 2560, 2565]
indicator_description: Discuss the causes of structural failure in construction [cite: 2565]
examplars:
1. Explain what is meant by structural failure. [cite: 2565]
2. Identify types of structural failures (cracks, splits, breakages). [cite: 2565]
3. Observe where structural failures occur in structures in the environment. [cite: 2565]
4. Discuss causes of structural failure (poor design, poor material, weak joint). [cite: 2565]
5. Discuss how static and dynamic forces can cause structures to fail. [cite: 2565]
6. Make sketches and notes on structural failures, in groups. [cite: 2565]
7. Display sketches for appraisal. [cite: 2565]

subject: Career Technology
sub_strand: SIMPLE STRUCTURES AND MECHANISMS, ELECTRIC AND ELECTRONIC SYSTEMS
content_standard: B7.4.1.1 Demonstrate understanding of structures in construction [cite: 2560, 2565, 2569]
indicator_description: Design and make simple structures [cite: 2569]
examplars:
1. Identify materials, tools and equipment for making structures. [cite: 2569]
2. Plan and design the artefact. [cite: 2569]
3. Make mock-ups of simple structures. [cite: 2569]
4. Test and evaluate the mock-ups by indicating strengths and weaknesses. [cite: 2569]
5. Display the mock-ups for appraisal. [cite: 2569]

subject: Career Technology
content_standard: B7.5.1.1 Demonstrate knowledge and skills of drawing materials, instruments, lines and their applications, and freehand sketching [cite: 2575]
indicator_description: Identify drawing materials, instruments and equipment used for Graphic Communication [cite: 2575]
examplars:
1. Identify drawing materials, instruments and equipment (paper, board, pencils, compasses). [cite: 2575]
2. Discuss the uses of drawing materials, tools and equipment. [cite: 2575]
3. Demonstrate appropriate uses and manipulation of instruments. [cite: 2575]
4. Demonstrate how to care for and maintain drawing materials and instruments. [cite: 2575]

subject: Career Technology
content_standard: B7.5.1.1 Demonstrate knowledge and skills of drawing materials, instruments, lines and their applications, and freehand sketching [cite: 2575, 2579]
indicator_description: Identify the types of lines used in graphic communication [cite: 2579]
examplars:
1. Identify lines (horizontal, vertical, continuous thick and thin). [cite: 2579]
2. Describe the features and uses of the lines. [cite: 2579]
3. Illustrate the applications of lines in drawn objects. [cite: 2579]
4. Make a chart on how the lines are applied in drawing. [cite: 2579]
5. Present the chart for appraisal. [cite: 2579]

subject: Career Technology
content_standard: B7.5.1.1 Demonstrate knowledge and skills of drawing materials, instruments, lines and their applications, and freehand sketching [cite: 2575, 2579]
indicator_description: Make sketches of lines, curves, objects, and write the letterings [cite: 2579]
examplars:
1. Identify lower and upper case letters. [cite: 2579]
2. Discuss the principles of lettering (Titles 8mm, general info 6mm). [cite: 2579]
3. Set out drawing paper and prepare title block. [cite: 2579]

subject: Career Technology
content_standard: B7.5.1.1 Demonstrate knowledge and skills of drawing materials, instruments, lines and their applications, and freehand sketching [cite: 2575, 2579, 2584]
indicator_description: Make sketches of objects [cite: 2584]
examplars:
1. Discuss what is meant by sketching. E.g. A quick way of putting ideas down freehand. [cite: 2584]
2. Illustrate the techniques of sketching objects in 2-D plane figures. [cite: 2584]
3. Illustrate the techniques of sketching objects in 3-D (isometric, oblique, perspective). [cite: 2584]
4. Practise sketching plane objects and pictorial objects to build an album. [cite: 2584]

subject: Career Technology
content_standard: B7.5.2.1 Demonstrate understanding of Designing [cite: 2590]
indicator_description: Work with a given design brief [cite: 2590]
examplars:
1. Study a given design brief to identify the problem. [cite: 2590]
2. Analyse the problem and list possible solution ideas. [cite: 2590]
3. Discuss ideas in groups. [cite: 2590]

subject: Career Technology
content_standard: B7.5.2.1 Demonstrate understanding of Designing [cite: 2590]
indicator_description: Generate ideas [cite: 2590]
examplars:
1. Use freehand sketching to generate three possible ideas and write descriptive notes. [cite: 2590]
2. Verify if the generated ideas satisfy the solution. [cite: 2590]
3. Select the best design and draw it in pictorial form. [cite: 2590]
4. Provide suitable dimensions to the selected design. [cite: 2590]
5. Prepare a simple working drawing (front elevation, plan, end view). [cite: 2590]

subject: Career Technology
content_standard: B7.5.3.1 Demonstrate understanding of planning for making artefacts/products [cite: 2595]
indicator_description: Discuss the factors to consider when planning a meal [cite: 2595]
examplars:
1. Discuss different types of meals served in a day. [cite: 2595]
2. Discuss factors to consider when planning meals (Nutritional needs, food available, budget). [cite: 2595]

subject: Career Technology
sub_strand: 4. MAKING ARTEFACTS FROM COMPLIANT, RESISTANT MATERIALS AND FOOD INGREDIENTS
content_standard: B7.5.4.1 Demonstrate skills of making artefacts/ products [cite: 2602]
indicator_description: Demonstrate skills in preparing food using moist methods of cooking [cite: 2602]
examplars:
1. Discuss reasons for cooking food. [cite: 2602]
2. Identify and classify moist methods (boiling, stewing, poaching). [cite: 2602]
3. Identify foods that can be boiled. [cite: 2602]
4. Discuss types of boiling (absorption, part of food, discarded). [cite: 2602]
5. Discuss advantages/disadvantages of boiling. [cite: 2602]
6. Describe principles for boiling. [cite: 2607]
7. Prepare a dish using boiling method. [cite: 2607]
8. Explain what is meant by stewing. [cite: 2607]
9. Identify foods for stewing. [cite: 2607]
10. Discuss principles and advantages/disadvantages of stewing. [cite: 2607]
11. Prepare a dish using stewing method. [cite: 2607]

subject: Career Technology
sub_strand: 4. MAKING ARTEFACTS FROM COMPLIANT, RESISTANT MATERIALS AND FOOD INGREDIENTS
content_standard: B7.5.4.1 Demonstrate skills of making artefacts/ products [cite: 2602, 2611]
indicator_description: Demonstrate skills of making artefacts/products in sewing and crocheting [cite: 2611]
examplars:
1. Make specimens of basic sewing stitches (tacking, running, back stitches). [cite: 2611]
2. Display specimens for appraisal. [cite: 2611]
3. Make specimens of basic crocheting stitches (chain, slip, double crochet). [cite: 2611]
4. Display specimens for appraisal. [cite: 2611]

subject: Career Technology
sub_strand: 4. MAKING ARTEFACTS FROM COMPLIANT, RESISTANT MATERIALS AND FOOD INGREDIENTS
content_standard: B7.5.4.1 Demonstrate skills of making artefacts/ products [cite: 2602, 2611]
indicator_description: Make mock ups using compliant materials [cite: 2611]
examplars:
1. Organise cardboard or paper. [cite: 2611]
2. Use cutting list and drawings to undertake measuring, marking and cutting. [cite: 2611]
3. Check dimensions for accurate surface development. [cite: 2611]
4. Fold parts to obtain artefact. [cite: 2611]
5. Use appropriate jointing materials and tools. [cite: 2611]
6. Apply suitable finish. [cite: 2611]
7. Test and evaluate for modifications. [cite: 2611]

subject: Career Technology
content_standard: B7.6.1.1 Demonstrate awareness of own learning styles, interests, biases, beliefs and reasons for pursuing Career Technology [cite: 2616]
indicator_description: Evaluate own learning styles, interests and reasons for pursuing Career Technology [cite: 2616]
examplars:
1. Think-pair-share own learning styles, interests and reasons for pursuing Career Technology. [cite: 2616]
2. Examine own biases and beliefs about vocations (e.g. gender-based course perceptions). [cite: 2616]

subject: Career Technology
sub_strand: 2. ESTABLISHING AND MANAGING A SMALL BUSINESS ENTERPRISE
content_standard: B7.6.2.1 Demonstrate understanding of Establishing and Managing a Small Business Enterprise [cite: 2624]
indicator_description: Explain what is meant by 'entrepreneurship' and 'entrepreneur' [cite: 2624]
examplars:
1. Entrepreneurship is the activity of setting up a business, taking financial risks in hope of profit. [cite: 2624]
2. An entrepreneur is a person who sets up a business. [cite: 2624]

subject: Career Technology
sub_strand: 2. ESTABLISHING AND MANAGING A SMALL BUSINESS ENTERPRISE
content_standard: B7.6.2.1 Demonstrate understanding of Establishing and Managing a Small Business Enterprise [cite: 2624]
indicator_description: Describe the characteristics of a successful entrepreneur [cite: 2624]
examplars:
1. Explain characteristics of an entrepreneur (creative, passionate, resourceful). [cite: 2624]
2. Discuss characteristics leading to success (opportunity seeking, goal setting, perseverance). [cite: 2624]

subject: Career Technology
sub_strand: 2. ESTABLISHING AND MANAGING A SMALL BUSINESS ENTERPRISE
content_standard: B7.6.2.1 Demonstrate understanding of Establishing and Managing a Small Business Enterprise [cite: 2624]
indicator_description: Explain the advantages and disadvantages of being an entrepreneur [cite: 2624]
examplars: 1. Discuss advantages (self-management) and disadvantages (irregular income). [cite: 2624]

subject: Career Technology
sub_strand: 2. ESTABLISHING AND MANAGING A SMALL BUSINESS ENTERPRISE
content_standard: B7.6.2.1 Demonstrate understanding of Establishing and Managing a Small Business Enterprise [cite: 2624, 2629]
indicator_description: Explain what is meant by Career Technology Entrepreneurship [cite: 2629]
examplars:
1. Explain what is meant by Career Technology entrepreneurship using pictures. [cite: 2629]
2. Give examples of trade areas (mechanical, catering, wood work). [cite: 2629]

subject: Career Technology
sub_strand: 2. ESTABLISHING AND MANAGING A SMALL BUSINESS ENTERPRISE
content_standard: B7.6.2.1 Demonstrate understanding of Establishing and Managing a Small Business Enterprise [cite: 2624, 2629]
indicator_description: Identify entrepreneurial opportunities in the locality [cite: 2629]
examplars:
1. Explore locality and interact with entrepreneurs. [cite: 2629]
2. Research opportunities in the locality. [cite: 2629]
3. Write names of popular enterprises. [cite: 2629]
4. Find out how business was started and challenges faced. [cite: 2629]
5. Write down findings for presentation. [cite: 2629]
